    91→@dataclass
    92→class Token:
    93→    type: TokenType
    94→    value: str

    98→    def __repr__(self):

   102→class Lexer:
   103→    def __init__(self, source: str):
   104→        self.source = source
   105→        self.pos = 0
   106→        self.line = 1
   107→        self.col = 1
   108→
   109→    def peek(self, offset=0) -> str:
   110→        p = self.pos + offset
   111→        return self.source[p] if p < len(self.source) else '\0'
   112→
   113→    def advance(self) -> str:
   114→        ch = self.peek()
   115→        if ch == '\n':
   116→            self.line += 1
   117→            self.col = 1
   118→        else:
   119→            self.col += 1
   120→        self.pos += 1
   121→        return ch
   122→
   123→    def skip_whitespace(self):
   124→        while self.peek() in ' \t\r':
   125→            self.advance()
   126→        if self.peek() == '\n':
   127→            self.advance()
   128→
   129→    def read_identifier(self) -> str:
   130→        start = self.pos
   131→        while self.peek().isalnum() or self.peek() == '_':
   132→            self.advance()
   133→        return self.source[start:self.pos]
   134→
   135→    def read_number(self) -> str:
   136→        start = self.pos
   137→        while self.peek().isdigit() or self.peek() == '.':
   138→            self.advance()
   139→        return self.source[start:self.pos]
   140→
   141→    def read_string(self) -> str:
   142→        quote = self.advance()
   143→        result = quote
   144→        while self.peek() != '\0' and self.peek() != quote:
   145→            if self.peek() == '\\':
   146→                result += self.advance()
   147→            result += self.advance()
   148→        if self.peek() == quote:
   149→            result += self.advance()
   150→        return result
   151→
   152→    def tokens(self) -> List[Token]:
   153→        result = []
   154→        while self.pos < len(self.source):
   155→            ch = self.peek()
   156→            if ch in ' \t\r':
   157→                self.skip_whitespace()
   158→                continue
   159→            if ch == '\n':
   160→                result.append(Token(TokenType.NEWLINE, '\n', self.line, self.col))
   161→                self.advance()
   162→                continue
   163→            if ch == '/' and self.peek(1) == '/':
   164→                while self.peek() not in '\n\0':
   165→                    self.advance()
   166→                continue
   167→
   168→            # String
   169→            if ch == '"':
   170→                result.append(Token(TokenType.STRING, self.read_string(), self.line, self.col))
   171→                continue
   172→
   173→            # Number
   174→            if ch.isdigit() or (ch == '.' and self.peek(1).isdigit()):
   175→                result.append(Token(TokenType.NUMBER, self.read_number(), self.line, self.col))
   176→                continue
   177→
   178→            # Identifier/Keyword
   179→            if ch.isalpha() or ch == '_':
   180→                ident = self.read_identifier()
   181→                ident_lower = ident.lower()
   182→                if ident_lower in KEYWORDS:
   183→                    result.append(Token(KEYWORDS[ident_lower], ident, self.line, self.col))
   184→                else:
   185→                    result.append(Token(TokenType.IDENTIFIER, ident, self.line, self.col))
   186→                continue
   187→
   188→            # Boolean literals
   189→            if ident := self.read_identifier() if self.peek().isalpha() else '':
   190→                if ident in ('TRUE', 'FALSE'):
   191→                    result.append(Token(TokenType.BOOL, ident, self.line, self.col))
   192→                    continue
   193→
   194→            # Operators
   195→            for op, tok_type in OP_SYMS.items():
   196→                if self.source.startswith(op, self.pos):
   197→                    result.append(Token(tok_type, op, self.line, self.col))
   198→                    self.pos += len(op)
   199→                    self.col += len(op)
   200→                    break
   201→            else:
   202→                raise ParseError(f"Unexpected char '{ch}' at line {self.line}")
   203→
   204→        result.append(Token(TokenType.EOF, '', self.line, self.col))
   205→        return result
   206→

   347→class Parser:
   348→    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token]):
   349→        self.tokens = tokens
   350→        self.pos = 0
   351→
   352→    def peek(self, offset=0) -> Token:
   353→        p = self.pos + offset
   354→        return self.tokens[p] if p < len(self.tokens) else self.tokens[-1]
   355→
   356→    def advance(self) -> Token:
   357→        tok = self.peek()
   358→        self.pos += 1
   359→        return tok
   360→
   361→    def expect(self, tok_type: TokenType, value: str = None) -> Token:
   362→        tok = self.advance()
   363→        if tok.type != tok_type:
   364→            raise ParseError(f"Expected {tok_type.name}, got {tok.type.name}")
   365→        if value and tok.value != value:
   366→            raise ParseError(f"Expected '{value}', got '{tok.value}'")
   367→        return tok
   368→
   369→    def parse_program(self) -> Program:
   370→        tasks = {}
   371→        while self.peek().type != TokenType.EOF:
   372→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.TASK:
   373→                t = self.parse_task()
   374→                tasks[t.name] = t
   375→            else:
   376→                raise ParseError(f"Unexpected {self.peek()}")
   377→        return Program(tasks)
   378→
   379→    def parse_task(self) -> TaskDef:
   380→        self.expect(TokenType.TASK)
   381→        name = self.expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value
   382→
   383→        params = []
   384→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.PARAMS:
   385→            self.expect(TokenType.PARAMS)
   386→            self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   387→            params = self.parse_params()
   388→            self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   389→
   390→        run = None
   391→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.RUN:
   392→            run = self.parse_run()
   393→
   394→        success = {}
   395→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.SUCCESS:
   396→            self.expect(TokenType.SUCCESS)
   397→            self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   398→            while self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
   399→                name = self.advance().value
   400→                self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   401→                expr = self.parse_expr()
   402→                self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   403→                success[name] = expr
   404→            self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   405→
   406→        requires = None
   407→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.REQUIRES:
   408→            self.expect(TokenType.REQUIRES)
   409→            requires = self.parse_expr()
   410→
   411→        output = None
   412→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.OUTPUT:
   413→            self.expect(TokenType.OUTPUT)
   414→            output = self.expect(TokenType.STRING).value.strip('"')
   415→
   416→        timeout = None
   417→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.TIMEOUT:
   418→            self.expect(TokenType.TIMEOUT)
   419→            timeout = float(self.expect(TokenType.NUMBER).value)
   420→
   421→        return TaskDef(name, params, run, success, requires, output, timeout)
   422→
   423→    def parse_params(self) -> List[ParamDef]:
   424→        params = []
   425→        while self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
   426→            name = self.advance().value
   427→            self.expect(TokenType.COLON)
   428→            type_str = self.expect(TokenType.IDENTIFIER).value
   429→            default = None
   430→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.ASSIGN:
   431→                self.advance()
   432→                default = self.parse_literal()
   433→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.SEMI:
   434→                self.advance()
   435→            params.append(ParamDef(name, type_str, default))
   436→        return params
   437→
   438→    def parse_run(self) -> RunBlock:
   439→        self.expect(TokenType.RUN)
   440→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACE)
   441→        commands = []
   442→        while self.peek().type != TokenType.RBRACE:
   443→            cmd = self.read_command()
   444→            if cmd.strip():
   445→                commands.append(cmd.strip())
   446→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACE)
   447→        return RunBlock(commands)
   448→
   449→    def read_command(self) -> str:
   450→        start = self.pos
   451→        brace_depth = 0
   452→        while self.pos < len(self.tokens):
   453→            tok = self.peek()
   454→            if tok.type == TokenType.LBRACE:
   455→                brace_depth += 1
   456→            elif tok.type == TokenType.RBRACE:
   457→                if brace_depth == 0:
   458→                    break
   459→                brace_depth -= 1
   460→            self.pos += 1
   461→        end = self.pos
   462→        return ''.join(t.value for t in self.tokens[start:end]).strip()
   463→
   464→    def parse_expr(self) -> Expr:
   465→        return self.parse_or()
   466→
   467→    def parse_or(self) -> Expr:
   468→        left = self.parse_and()
   469→        while self.peek().type == TokenType.OR:
   470→            self.advance()
   471→            right = self.parse_and()
   472→            left = BinOpExpr('||', left, right)
   473→        return left
   474→
   475→    def parse_and(self) -> Expr:
   476→        left = self.parse_cmp()
   477→        while self.peek().type == TokenType.AND:
   478→            self.advance()
   479→            right = self.parse_cmp()
   480→            left = BinOpExpr('&&', left, right)
   481→        return left
   482→
   483→    def parse_cmp(self) -> Expr:
   484→        left = self.parse_add()
   485→        while self.peek().type in (TokenType.EQ, TokenType.NEQ,
   486→                                    TokenType.LT, TokenType.GT,
   487→                                    TokenType.LTE, TokenType.GTE):
   488→            op = self.advance().value
   489→            right = self.parse_add()
   490→            left = BinOpExpr(op, left, right)
   491→        return left
   492→
   493→    def parse_add(self) -> Expr:
   494→        left = self.parse_mul()
   495→        while self.peek().type in (TokenType.PLUS, TokenType.MINUS):
   496→            op = self.advance().value
   497→            right = self.parse_mul()
   498→            left = BinOpExpr(op, left, right)
   499→        return left
   500→
   501→    def parse_mul(self) -> Expr:
   502→        left = self.parse_unary()
   503→        while self.peek().type in (TokenType.MULT, TokenType.DIV, TokenType.MOD):
   504→            op = self.advance().value
   505→            right = self.parse_unary()
   506→            left = BinOpExpr(op, left, right)
   507→        return left
   508→
   509→    def parse_unary(self) -> Expr:
   510→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.NOT:
   511→            self.advance()
   512→            return UnOpExpr('!', self.parse_unary())
   513→        if self.peek().type in (TokenType.PLUS, TokenType.MINUS):
   514→            op = self.advance().value
   515→            return UnOpExpr(op, self.parse_unary())
   516→        return self.parse_primary()
   517→
   518→    def parse_primary(self) -> Expr:
   519→        tok = self.peek()
   520→
   521→        if tok.type == TokenType.LPAREN:
   522→            self.advance()
   523→            expr = self.parse_expr()
   524→            self.expect(TokenType.RPAREN)
   525→            return expr
   526→
   527→        if tok.type == TokenType.BOOL:
   528→            self.advance()
   529→            return LiteralExpr(tok.value == 'TRUE')
   530→
   531→        if tok.type == TokenType.NUMBER:
   532→            self.advance()
   533→            val = tok.value
   534→            if '.' in val:
   535→                return LiteralExpr(float(val))
   536→            return LiteralExpr(int(val))
   537→
   538→        if tok.type == TokenType.STRING:
   539→            self.advance()
   540→            return LiteralExpr(tok.value.strip('"'))
   541→
   542→        if tok.type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER:
   543→            ident = self.advance().value
   544→
   545→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.LPAREN:
   546→                return self.parse_func_call(ident)
   547→
   548→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.LBRACK:
   549→                return self.parse_index(ident)
   550→
   551→            return VarExpr(ident)
   552→
   553→        raise ParseError(f"Unexpected {tok}")
   554→
   555→    def parse_func_call(self, name: str) -> FuncCallExpr:
   556→        self.expect(TokenType.LPAREN)
   557→        args = []
   558→        kwargs = {}
   559→        while self.peek().type != TokenType.RPAREN:
   560→            arg = self.parse_arg()
   561→            if isinstance(arg, tuple):
   562→                kwargs[arg[0]] = arg[1]
   563→            else:
   564→                args.append(arg)
   565→            if self.peek().type == TokenType.COMMA:
   566→                self.advance()
   567→        self.expect(TokenType.RPAREN)
   568→        return FuncCallExpr(name, args, kwargs)
   569→
   570→    def parse_arg(self) -> Any:
   571→        if self.peek().type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER and self.peek(1).type == TokenType.EQ:
   572→            name = self.advance().value
   573→            self.expect(TokenType.EQ)
   574→            return (name, self.parse_expr())
   575→        return self.parse_expr()
   576→
   577→    def parse_index(self, name: str) -> IndexExpr:
   578→        self.expect(TokenType.LBRACK)
   579→        idx = self.parse_expr()
   580→        self.expect(TokenType.RBRACK)
   581→        return IndexExpr(VarExpr(name), idx)
   582→
   583→    def parse_literal(self) -> Any:
   584→        tok = self.peek()
   585→        if tok.type in (TokenType.BOOL,):
   586→            self.advance()
   587→            return tok.value == 'TRUE'
   588→        if tok.type == TokenType.NUMBER:
   589→            self.advance()
   590→            val = tok.value
   591→            return float(val) if '.' in val else int(val)
   592→        if tok.type == TokenType.STRING:
   593→            self.advance()
   594→            return tok.value.strip('"')
